title: scRNAseq analysis
text: The Parse pipeline handles the initial processing of combinatorial indexing single-cell gene expression data. It takes raw FASTQs and outputs a Digital Gene Expression (DGE) matrix.

There wass a clash between the snakemake environment and the spipe environment. Workaround: We manually activate spipe-1.3.1 within the shell block rather than using the conda: directive. :::

title: Project Overview
text:
sn-eQTL Analysis Workflow
This project processes raw FASTQ files into cell-type-specific eQTL discoveries.

Workflow Summary
The pipeline is divided into 13 main modules: 1. 01 Parse: Raw data processing and alignment. 2. 02 Scanpy: snRNAseq QC and clustering 3. 03 Genotyping (pre-imputation): Genotype QC and harmonisation for TOPMED imputation 4. 04 Genotyping (post-imputation): Genotype QC post-imputation 5. 05 TensorQTL: eQTL Discovery and QC 6. 06 eQTL replication: pi1 Testing etc. 7. 07 Prep GWAS: Download and munge GWAS data for downstream applications 8. 08 SuSiE finemapping: Generate finemapped SNPs for MaxCPP annotation 9. 09 S-LDSR: Run Stratified LDSR 10. 10 SMR: Run Summary Mendelian Randomisation 11. 11 TWAS-weights: Generate TWAS weights for cTWAS 12. 12 cTWAS: Run Causal TWAS 13. 12 Visualisation: Generate figures and tables for manuscript

title: Parse Alignment
text:
Overview
This pipeline performs combinatorial indexing processing using the Parse Biosciences split-pipe (v1.3.1) software. It orchestrates the transition from raw FASTQ multiplexed libraries to a Digital Gene Expression (DGE) matrix.



Workflow Logic
The pipeline follows a tiered execution strategy to manage heavy I/O and storage constraints.





graph TD
    A[get_refs] --> B[make_ref]
    C[cat_fqs] --> D[run_parse]
    B --> D
    D --> E[run_parse_combine]

    style D fill:#f96,stroke:#333
    style E fill:#f96,stroke:#333







get_refs: Download the hg38 reference files (Ensembl Release 113).
make_ref: Prepares genome indices for split-pipe.
cat_fqs: Combine fastq files per sublibrary (across lanes), per read (R1, R2).
run_parse: Core alignment and barcode demultiplexing.
run_parse_combine: Aggregates sublibraries into a unified plate-level DGE.








Warning



Environment Clash Workaround
There was a conflict between the Snakemake YAML parser and the spipe environment. Fix: The spipe-1.3.1 environment is manually activated within the shell block of the rule rather than via the Snakemake conda: directive.




Requirements



Category
Requirement




Software
Parse split-pipe v1.3.1


Environment
spipe-1.3.1


Reference
ENSEMBL Release 113 (hg38)


Inputs
Demultiplexed FASTQs, Sample List


Metadata
JSON path map (via scripts/create_parse_json.py)


Disk Space
~3-5 TB per plate (Scratch)






Library Composition & Throughput
We processed 150 samples across three sequencing plates:

Sublibraries: 43 total (Plate 1: 15, Plate 2: 16, Plate 3: 12).
FASTQ Depth: Each sublibrary consists of 24 files (4 lanes × 3 runs × 2 reads).
Data Volume: ~3.0 TB of final raw output (~1 TB per plate).




Resource Management Strategy
To navigate a 5TB storage quota and heavy memory requirements, the following engineering strategies were implemented:

Concurrency Control: Restricted to 3 parallel jobs to prevent storage overflow.
I/O Optimization: Combined FASTQ transfer and concatenation into a single step to minimize scratch disk residency.
Cleanup Logic: Intermediate merged FASTQs were deleted immediately upon successful alignment.
HPC Allocation: Executed on DPMCN nodes (c_compute_neuro1) to avoid prohibitive queue times on Hawk.




Compute profile

Threads: 32
Memory: 360 GB
Walltime: 10 Days




Known Issue
There wass a clash between the snakemake environment and the spipe environment. Workaround: We manually activate spipe-1.3.1 within the shell block rather than using the conda directive.
